notes_on_abnormal_findings.notes_line_1: Item 44: Applicant has a well-healed 4 cm linear scar on the anterior aspect of the left shin, approximately 6 cm above the ankle. Scar is flat, non-tender, with no associated functional limitation or underlying bony deformity. Applicant reports this resulted from a childhood bicycle accident at age 11 when he fell onto a curb; no fracture occurred, and there has been no subsequent issue with walking, running, or weight-bearing activities.
notes_on_abnormal_findings.notes_line_2: No other significant scars, tattoos, or identifying marks noted on exposed areas during the examination. No keloid formation or evidence of chronic skin disease associated with the scar. The mark is documented solely as an identifying feature and is not considered medically significant for flight duties.

vision_assessment_items_50_54.mode_used: Ishihara pseudoisochromatic plates (24-plate edition), all plates correctly identified on first attempt under standard illumination.

vital_signs_and_laboratory_tests_items_5.blood_pressure_sitting_mm_hg: 118/72 mm Hg sitting in right arm, standard adult cuff, measured after 5 minutes of rest.
vital_signs_and_laboratory_tests_items_5.other_tests_given_line_1: Routine dipstick urinalysis performed in office: negative for protein, glucose, ketones, blood, nitrites, and leukocyte esterase; specific gravity and pH within normal limits.
vital_signs_and_laboratory_tests_items_5.other_tests_given_line_2: No resting ECG indicated based on age (32), absence of cardiac symptoms, normal cardiovascular exam, and no personal or family history of premature cardiac disease.

comments_on_history_and_findings_item_60.comments_history_findings_line_1: Applicant is a 32-year-old male commercial pilot applicant with no significant past medical history, no history of hospitalizations or surgeries other than wisdom tooth extraction at age 19 without complications, and no known drug allergies. Denies history of asthma, diabetes, hypertension, seizures, loss of consciousness, or psychiatric illness. Non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially (1–2 drinks on weekends), denies any history of substance misuse or DUI.
comments_on_history_and_findings_item_60.comments_history_findings_line_2: Physical examination today is unremarkable aside from a minor, well-healed scar on the left shin (see Item 44 notes). Cardiovascular, respiratory, neurologic, and psychiatric assessments are all within normal limits. Vision and hearing exceed minimum standards without need for correction. BMI is approximately 24.7 kg/m², consistent with a normal weight range for height.
comments_on_history_and_findings_item_60.comments_history_findings_line_3: Applicant reports regular exercise (running 3–4 times per week for 30–40 minutes and light strength training twice weekly) and generally balanced diet. No reported episodes of in-flight incapacitation, disorientation, or performance-impairing symptoms during flight training. Sleep pattern is regular (approximately 7–8 hours per night) with no symptoms suggestive of sleep apnea or other sleep disorders.
comments_on_history_and_findings_item_60.comments_history_findings_line_4: Based on the history provided and findings on examination, there are no identified medical conditions that would be expected to interfere with the safe exercise of pilot duties. Applicant appears motivated, health-conscious, and appropriately informed about the importance of self-grounding if any significant future medical issues or medication changes arise.

medical_examiners_declaration_and_contac.address: SkyCare Aviation Medical Clinic, 4820 Meridian Ave Suite 310, Seattle, WA 98108
